dialogues.text: The pharmacy keeps calling the house. I've been covering it for weeks out of the grocery money, and I just — I need thirty. I hate that I need thirty.
dialogues.yes_response: Thank you. I didn't know how to ask. I never seem to know how to ask you for anything.
dialogues.no_response: No. Of course. I'll sort something out. I always sort something out eventually.

dialogues.text: The car's making that sound again, the low one. I've started being scared to drive it after dark. Can you help me with it? Thirty would cover the look-over.
dialogues.yes_response: That's a relief. I've been putting it off. I keep putting everything off lately, I've noticed.
dialogues.no_response: It's probably nothing. A belt, or my nerves. I'll wait a little longer and see.

dialogues.text: There's a bill that came in a window envelope. I don't want to go into the details of it, I just want it to stop existing. I need thirty.
dialogues.yes_response: Thank you. That's one less thing on the pile. I have far too many things on the pile.
dialogues.no_response: I'll sort it. Don't worry about me, sweetheart. I'll sort it. That's my job, not yours.

dialogues.text: I haven't been sleeping. I lie there and I think, and the thinking has a direction I don't like. I just need thirty and then I'll stop bothering you with all this.
dialogues.yes_response: You're good. You've always been good, since you were small. I don't say that to you nearly enough.
dialogues.no_response: No. Don't worry. I shouldn't have said any of that part. Forget the part that wasn't about money.

dialogues.text: I want to get something nice for the house. For when people come by — so it looks like a place where things are alright. I'd need thirty for it.
dialogues.yes_response: I want things to look right when they visit. I need it to feel like it used to feel in here.
dialogues.no_response: It can wait. Things don't have to be perfect for people. I keep forgetting that.

dialogues.text: I keep starting sentences I don't have endings for. I do it all day now, to the cat, to the kettle. Can you just — thirty, and I won't try to finish this one.
dialogues.yes_response: Thank you. I don't want to talk about the rest of it. Not today. Today let's just do the thirty.
dialogues.no_response: Okay. That's okay. I'll work it out. The sentence and the thirty, both.

dialogues.text: I called you three times last week. I know how busy you are, I'm not counting it against you. I just need thirty, and then you can get back to your day.
dialogues.yes_response: That's all I needed. I'm sorry I made it into a whole phone call. I love you. Go on.
dialogues.no_response: No, I know. I know you're dealing with more than you say. I do know that. Go.

dialogues.text: I don't know what to say to you anymore. I don't know how to be useful to you. Thirty is a thing I understand — can you spare thirty?
dialogues.yes_response: Thank you. I'll figure out the rest of it. The not-knowing-what-to-say part. I always do, eventually.
dialogues.no_response: Okay. I know. It's okay. You don't have to carry my part of it too.

dialogues.text: I bought you something. I couldn't afford it, and I bought it anyway, and now I need thirty back or it bounces. Please don't ask me why I did it. I don't have a good answer.
dialogues.yes_response: I saw it in the window and I thought of you and I just — I'm sorry. I love you more than is sensible.
dialogues.no_response: I'll return it. It was a foolish thing to do. I don't know what came over me, standing there.

dialogues.text: Just thirty. Please. I don't want to explain it and I don't want to dress it up. I love you, and I need thirty, and today those are the only two things I'm sure of.
dialogues.yes_response: Thank you. I love you. You know I love you. You've always known, even when I was no good at showing it.
dialogues.no_response: I know. It's okay. I know. Come by tomorrow if you can. Just to sit.
